page.content: In the FY2023 report for AB InBev within the Asia-Pacific region, key performance indicators reveal a Distribution Efficiency of 90%, with Logistics Metrics indicating a Cost Ratio of 8.5 and Sales Performance metrics showing a Current Assets figure of 1.2.

page.content: The Inventory Turnover stands at 0.9, complemented by a Delivery Rate of 35% and an Order Fulfillment rate of 15%. The SQL Ratio is 1.5, with an On-Time Delivery rate of 95%, while Freight Costs total 20%. Sales Growth is reported at 12%, with a Market Share of 30% and an Operational Efficiency of 85%, alongside an Average Lead Time of 5 days.

lines.text: The Inventory Turnover stands at 0.9, complemented by a Delivery Rate of 35% and an Order Fulfillment rate of 15%. The
SQL Ratio is 1.5, with an On-Time Delivery rate of 95%, while Freight Costs total 20%. Sales Growth is reported at 12%, with

page.content: Total Expenses are ₩150B, while Total Revenue amounts to ₩300B, leading to a Net Income of ₩50B. Total Assets are valued at ₩400B against Total Liabilities of ₩250B, resulting in Shareholder Equity of ₩150B. In the product category analysis, Plant-Based Beverages report a Volume of 50,000 L and Revenue of 200,000 KRW for Q1, while Plant-Based Snacks show a Volume of 10,000,000 kg and Revenue of 30,000 KRW. For Q2, the figures rise to 75,000 L and 250,000 KRW for beverages, and 15,000,000 kg and 35,000 KRW for snacks. Finally, in Q3, volumes and revenues continue to increase, reaching 100,000 L and 300,000 KRW for beverages, and 20,000,000 kg and 40,000 KRW for snacks, culminating in a Total Plant-Based Revenue of 15,000,000 KRW and a Market Share of 30%.
